steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage states some expressions appear only in songs, while current preservation efforts focus on spoken language. The question asks what logically follows — the answer must bridge that gap.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — analyze musical similarities in Warlpiri songs and Karen hta songs of the Hin Lad Nai people.: Proposes analyzing songs for something other than preservation. Does not solve the preservation gap. ✗
  • Choice B — acknowledge that Indigenous songs are largely but not entirely composed using phrases taken from spoken language.: Acknowledges songs exist but takes no action toward preserving their unique expressions. ✗
  • Choice C — incorporate the preservation of songs into their broader efforts to protect Indigenous languages.: Directly closes the gap — if songs contain expressions not found in speech, adding songs to preservation efforts captures all expressions. ✓
  • Choice D — recognize that Indigenous languages likely have more unique expressions that represent ecological knowledge than represent other types of information.: Makes a claim the passage does not support. ✗
That is the correct answer.
